description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with patterned shingles and a dark brown brick exterior. The structure is composed of two intersecting volumes, one larger and one smaller, both with matching rooflines. Sparse, leafless tree branches are integrated into the scene, giving the house a slightly overgrown, abandoned appearance. The windows are few and asymmetrically placed, with a pair of rectangular windows visible on one side. The house sits on a patch of uneven green terrain, suggesting a yard or garden area. The overall design is simple, with minimal decorative elements and a utilitarian, rustic feel.
description: A dark brown brick house with a steep gable roof, sparse windows, and overgrown leafless branches, set on a patch of green terrain.
description_view_0: The house is seen from above, showing the intersecting gable roofs and the larger volume at the back, with tree branches encroaching on the structure.
description_view_1: A side view highlights the tall, narrow gable end and the dense cluster of branches against the brick wall.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the height of the main structure and the smaller attached volume, with branches visible on both sides.
description_view_3: The rear or side view reveals two rectangular windows and the continuation of the gable roof, with branches and green terrain visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple main door with no visible porch or decorative elements, possibly accessed directly from the ground level.
